title: Xenial Turkish Chocolate Dumplings
description: A springy, cultured Turkish dessert that blends dark chocolate with a mirepoix-infused dough, finished in a yogurt‑spiced sauce for an anti‑inflammatory solo dinner.
instructions: Combine flour, salt, and olive oil in a bowl, then stir in warm water until a soft dough forms.
Fold in chopped dark chocolate and knead for 5 minutes until smooth and springy.
Cover the dough and let it rest for 10 minutes at room temperature.
In a separate skillet, sauté diced carrot, celery, and onion in a little olive oil until softened, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the mirepoix to a small saucepan and simmer with ground ginger and turmeric for 2 minutes to create a fragrant sauce base.
Shape the rested dough into small dumpling pockets and fill each with a teaspoon of the mirepoix blend.
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil, then cook dumplings for 4 minutes until they rise to the surface.
Drain the dumplings and toss them with plain Greek yogurt, honey, and a pinch of salt, stirring until the sauce coats each dumpling.
Serve hot, garnished with a drizzle of melted dark chocolate and a sprinkle of fresh mint if desired.
